content.problemStatement: There is a big staircase with NNN steps (numbered 111 through NNN) in ChefLand. Let's denote the height of the top of step iii by hih_ihi‚Äã. Chef Ada is currently under the staircase at height 000 and she wants to reach the top of the staircase (the top of the last step). However, she can only jump from height hih_ihi‚Äã to the top of a step at height hfh_fhf‚Äã if hf‚àíhi‚â§Kh_f-h_i \le Khf‚Äã‚àíhi‚Äã‚â§K. To help Ada, we are going to construct some intermediate steps; each of them may be constructed between any two steps, or before the first step, and have any height. What is the minimum number of steps that need to be added to the staircase so that Ada could reach the top?
constraints: hi<hi+1h_i \lt h_{i+1}hi‚Äã<hi+1‚Äã for each valid iii
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line contains NNN space-seperated integers h1,h2,‚Ä¶,hNh_1, h_2, \dots, h_Nh1‚Äã,h2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,hN‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äî the minimum required number of steps.
